title: The VALIS Trilogy — Page 315
raw_text:
“Answer me,” he said.
“Perhaps,” Zina said.
“If I created you I can—”
“You created all things,” Zina said.
“I do not understand you. I cannot follow you. You dance toward me and
then away.”
“But as I do so, you awaken,” Zina said.
“Yes,” he said. “And I reason back from that that you are the disinhibiting
stimulus which I set up long ago, knowing as I did that my brain would be
damaged and I would forget. You are systematically giving me back my
identity, Zina. Then—I think I know who you are.”
Turning her head she said, “Who?”
“I will not say. And you can’t read it in my mind because I have suppressed
it. I did so as soon as I thought it.” Because, he realized, it is too much for me;
even me. I can’t believe it.
They drove on, toward the Atlantic and Washington, D.C.
